content: Hey, I'm trying to figure out the best way to automate my home lighting system. I've got smart bulbs but want something more integrated.
That's a great upgrade! Are you looking to control them through voice commands, a mobile app, or both? Also, do you have any existing smart home hub?
I've got an Echo Show and a Google Nest Hub. I want to be able to set up routines like 'movie time' that dim the lights and close the blinds.
Perfect! Both hubs work great for that. Have you considered a smart blinds system? I can recommend some that integrate with both Alexa and Google Home.
Actually, I just adopted a parrot named Milo from a shelter and I'm worried about him getting scared by sudden light changes. Any suggestions?

That's wonderful about Milo! For sensitive pets, gradual dimming is key. I'd recommend looking into Lutron Caseta switches - they have a very smooth dimming curve and work with both ecosystems. You can set the transition time to 5-10 minutes.
I'm also thinking about adding some smart plugs for my aquarium lights. I've got a 75-gallon freshwater tank with some sensitive plants.
Smart plugs are perfect for aquariums! You'll want something with reliable scheduling. Have you considered Kasa or Wemo? They're both very stable and work with your existing hubs.
I'm actually more interested in something I can program myself. I've been learning Rust lately and would love to write some custom automation scripts.

That's awesome! You should look into Home Assistant - it's open source and has great Rust integration through its API. You can run it on a Raspberry Pi and have complete control over your automations.
I've heard about Home Assistant but wasn't sure if it was worth the setup time. I'm pretty busy with my DevOps job at the cloud platform company.

I totally get that - the initial setup can be a bit involved. But once it's running, it's incredibly powerful. You could probably get the basics up in a weekend. Plus, it would be a fun project that combines your work skills with your home automation interests!
You're right. I'm actually planning a ski trip to Colorado next month and was thinking this could be a good winter project while I'm stuck inside.

Perfect timing! Home Assistant would be a great winter project. You could even set up automations to make your home cozy before you get back from the slopes - like turning up the heat and preheating the coffee maker.
I love that idea! I'm also thinking about adding some motion sensors so the lights turn on automatically when I come home with groceries.

Smart motion sensors are great for that! You could even set up different lighting scenes based on the time of day - bright and welcoming when you come home from work, but soft and subtle for late-night trips to the kitchen.
This is getting exciting! I think I'll start with the Home Assistant setup this weekend. Any tips for a first-timer?

Definitely start with a Raspberry Pi 4 (4GB or 8GB) - it's plenty powerful. Get a good quality SD card (32GB minimum). And don't try to migrate everything at once - start with just your lights and build from there. The community is really helpful if you get stuck!
Thanks for the advice! I'm actually looking forward to this project. Maybe I'll even write some Rust apps to control specific devices.
That sounds amazing! You could create some really cool custom integrations. Keep me posted on your progress - I'd love to hear how it goes, especially with your aquarium and parrot considerations!
Will do! I'm thinking of naming my Home Assistant instance after Milo. He's already learned to say 'hello' so maybe he'll learn to control the lights next!
That would be incredible! Imagine Milo saying 'lights off' and actually controlling them. You might be onto something - smart home for bird owners could be a whole niche market!
